openQuestions.text: What rate limits apply, and do they differ between free and paid tiers?
openQuestions.note: CONFIRMED UNDOCUMENTED in all authoritative sources (skill repo + refero.design SPA). Unverified gap — resolve via live probe in a later phase.

openQuestions.text: What is the free vs paid gating — which tools/capabilities/result volumes are free vs paid-only?
openQuestions.note: Only signal: craft knowledge = no account; live MCP = auth required. Exact tier boundaries UNDOCUMENTED. Unverified gap.

resolvedQuestions.text: What tools does the Refero MCP server expose (names, parameters, response shapes) for UI-design-reference search across apps, screens, flows, elements?
What auth model does the Refero MCP expect through mcp-remote (anonymous, OAuth, API key)?
resolvedQuestions.answer: Two paths: mcp-remote OAuth browser flow (existing manual, empty env, interactive first-use) OR Bearer token via HTTP header; endpoint https://api.refero.design/mcp

resolvedQuestions.text: What conventions does the refero_skill repo document that the mcp-refero transport packet should mirror?
How should a read-only Code-Mode-only transport packet be authored atop the existing mcp-remote manual, paired with sk-design judgment?
resolvedQuestions.answer: Simpler read-only-only peer of mcp-figma (MCP-only, no CLI); SKILL.md + references(refero_mcp_tools, tool_surface collapsed to read-only, mcp_wiring, troubleshooting) + assets(utcp_refero_manual, env_template); Code Mode naming refero.refero_<tool>; pairs with sk-design for judgment

keyFindings.text: 8 read-only tools across 3 layers: styles (search_styles, get_style), screens (search_screens, get_screen, get_similar_screens, get_screen_image), flows (search_flows, get_flow)

Documented client mistakes: no old _tool names, no get_design_guidance, no image_size/include_similar on get_screen, no limit/image_size/include_similar on search tools, response_format only where schema shows it
Auth: Bearer-token HTTP transport OR mcp-remote OAuth browser flow; endpoint https://api.refero.design/mcp; existing manual uses mcp-remote with empty env
Coverage limit: styles are web-marketing-scoped only (no dashboards/auth/settings/iOS as style systems); screens+flows support platform web|ios
refero_skill is a standalone design-methodology skill (peer of sk-design), NOT an mcp-tooling transport; MIT; SKILL.md + references/ + assets/
Existing .utcp_config.json manual is a minimal stdio-over-mcp-remote bridge, empty env; transport packet authored atop it read-only

NEGATIVE KNOWLEDGE: free-vs-paid gating and rate limits are NOT publicly documented — refero.design root/mcp/pricing are all SPAs (title only); skill repo documents neither. Only signal: craft knowledge no account, live MCP requires auth.

Auth lifecycle: path A mcp-remote OAuth (interactive browser sign-in first-use, token cached, empty env); path B Bearer token (HTTP header; token-grant flow undocumented). Code Mode env-prefix rule: refero_<NAME> if token wired via env. First-use browser sign-in is an operational constraint (headless/CI needs path B).

Transport-packet layout: mcp-refero is a SIMPLER read-only-only peer of mcp-figma (MCP-only, no CLI, no gating taxonomy). Files: SKILL.md + references/{refero_mcp_tools, tool_surface(read-only-only), mcp_wiring, troubleshooting} + assets/{utcp_refero_manual, env_template}. Code Mode calling: refero.refero_<tool>.

Layer-discipline routing rule (transport must surface): styles first (visual direction) -> screens (concrete UI) -> flows (journeys); combine layers; styles web-marketing-scoped caveat. Judgment routed to sk-design; transport never duplicates refero_skill methodology.

ruledOutDirections.approach: Scrape the JS-rendered body of refero.design/mcp for structured pricing/gating data
ruledOutDirections.reason: SPA returns only a title via WebFetch; no crawlable body

ruledOutDirections.approach: Further scraping of refero.design/* SPA routes (root, pricing) for pricing/gating
ruledOutDirections.reason: All routes yield only the title; pricing/gating facts are not web-crawlable
